notes: Tested against three hostile lenses. (1) 'This is just personal-brand-management dressed in Sanskrit' — false; the falsifiability paragraph explicitly distinguishes the verse from performance-theatre, popularity, conference-circuit visibility, and personal-brand-management. The verse is restricted to 'what the next senior engineer who reviews your work will model you as, after observing what you did when it was hard.' (2) 'No one really updates their model of an engineer based on one mid-cutover withdrawal' — observably false; the senior engineering hiring loop is full of 'I worked with X' anecdotes that propagate exactly this kind of signal across 18-24 month windows. (3) 'The named tools (Postgres, Kafka, Pulsar, GitHub, conference talks, open-source rate-limiter) are decorative' — false, they form the realistic shape of a senior IC's reputation surface. The doctrinal claim — that conduct under load is the long-lived signal peers use to model you — is signal-theory in Sanskrit, as the engineering layer correctly names it. PASS.
